title: The Nineteenth Century And After
content:
Though the great song return no more
There's keen delight in what we have:
The rattle of pebbles on the shore
Under the receding wave.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Butler Yeats, titled "The Nineteenth Century And After", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
